mentions.note: CEO Dave Rich (Kramer's favorite); oral GLP-1 launching spring 2026; potential beneficiary if Trump tariffs Novo Nordisk's Ozempic; strong competitive position.
Cramer's favorite pharma CEO Dave Rich; Eli Lilly launching oral GLP-1 in spring 2026; not going against owning Novo but bullish on Lilly competition in GLP-1 space.
Dominating GLP-1 market with strong pill form pipeline. David Ricks executing flawlessly. Preferred over Novo Nordisk in caller discussion.
Stock driven by GLP-1 clinical trial reports, not earnings; potential for new data announcement to get stock rolling.

GLP-1 pill Zounandea exceeding expectations with 20K+ users and strong demand despite late start; astounded market with fantastic quarter; strong for economy but healthcare not good stock market indicator.
Cramer did extensive research and is ready to roll; real bull market opportunity; very high conviction GLP-1 obesity drug platform and pipeline.
Pharma winner today; among sectors benefiting from rotation out of overheated data center names; healthcare sector solid.

Diabetes/weight-loss GLP-1 drugs surging; new retatrutide weight-loss data excellent (fat reduction without muscle loss); gene-therapy cholesterol breakthrough. Only pharma to crack trillion-dollar club; brilliant management.

Multiple GLP-1 formulations; pending drug sheds fat without muscle loss, offering potential knockout punch vs. Novo Nordisk if approved. Concern: overlap with Gtech growth holders may cause volatility.

Retatrutide shows superior weight loss; VERVE-102 gene therapy potential one-time cholesterol treatment; vaccine acquisitions diversify beyond GLP-1; CVS deal expands access; robust core pharma portfolio.
Cramer favorite; only healthcare bright spot for months; healthcare sector now broadening beyond just Lilly.
Earnings and drugs coming through; momentum building; massive GLP-1 production capacity built out; Cramer wants huge position for his charitable trust.
Earnings coming through, GLP-1 drugs ramping, new factories built; want huge position; stay in stock despite high share price.
Has already run a great deal but Cramer still likes it, even as he prefers J&J as the better relative entry right now.
Preferred over Novo Nordisk because Eli Lilly makes great drugs; growth story more attractive than Novo's price-cutting defense.
Caller James (GA) holds position up 20%; Cramer: never mind taking profits, prefer hold; one of travel trust favorites; would buy more if hit.
Market in 'giving up' moment on one of greatest drug companies in history; GLP-1 pill will be gigantic; buy this 'giving up' dislocation.

Tirzepatide pill approved—game-changing convenience vs. Novo; acquired Synaptic for $7.8B targeting narcolepsy/neurological disorders via orexin; weight-loss revenue wisely deployed; own for charitable trust.
Disappointed with new weight loss drug; stock sold off; Cramer says calm down—Lilly will tell good story on Thursday morning earnings with longer-term view than 10 days.
